nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Information
This workflow automatically creates a playlist in Spotify named "Downloads". It keeps a list of a defined amount of the latest liked songs up to date.

This enables only the Downloads playlist to set for automatic downloading and thus free up space on the device.

**Beware that it can take several minutes until a newly created Spotify developer app is fully functioning.**

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
## Information
This workflow automatically creates a playlist in Spotify named "Downloads". It keeps a list of a defined amount of the latest liked songs up to date.

This enables only the Downloads playlist to set for automatic downloading and thus free up space on the device.

**Beware that it can take several minutes until a newly created Spotify developer app is fully functioning.**

## Set Globals
Define the `download_limit` of how many songs should be kept in the Downloads playlist.
*This setup currently supports a maximum of 50.*

## Setup Trigger
Define the update interval. By default the playlist gets updated ones a day.
Get the "Downloads" playlist by name. Create it, if it does not exist.
Get all latest liked songs and check whether they already exist in the Downloads playlist.
Add new tracks to the Downloads playlist. Remove tracks if they exceed the defined limit.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: spotify  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "If no playlist" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Downloads Playlist" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Liked Tracks" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Loop Over Items" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitInBatches) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Aggregate" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Add tracks to Downloads" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Downloads Playlist" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Remove oldest tracks from Downloads" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Filter out Downloads Playlist" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Updated Downloads Playlist" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all Playlists" node (n8n-nodes-base.spotify) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "If no Downloads Playlist found" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Globals".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
